---
title: "*Dew Your Bubbles Have Flash?* (Raoult's Law, Dew Point, Bubble Point, Flash Calculations) Reference Page"
author: "DOFPro group"
format: html
html-math-method: mathjax
---
::: {.callout-important title="Still Under Construction!"}
This web page is just a bare outline. The complete details have not been filled in yet.
:::
# Intro to *Dew Your Bubbles Have Flash?*

Main title explanation

## Part 1

Video 1 explanation

<iframe width="560" height="315" src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/R4bimpGAV7o?si=IFTJpCzdfwOmqsIj" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" referrerpolicy="strict-origin-when-cross-origin" allowfullscreen></iframe>
[Visuals](../../NotYetHere.qmd)

## Part 2

Video  explanation

<iframe width="560" height="315" src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/9GyrfJzg-tg?si=tIbrZXZORXHYH_B2" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" referrerpolicy="strict-origin-when-cross-origin" allowfullscreen></iframe>
[Visuals](../../NotYetHere.qmd)

## Part 3

Video  explanation

<iframe width="560" height="315" src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/bG15qQ9gB4c?si=V6SC6uhwQXHIlHKt" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" referrerpolicy="strict-origin-when-cross-origin" allowfullscreen></iframe>
[Visuals](../../NotYetHere.qmd)

# Examples and Definitions

## Examples Links

## Definitions

Raoult's Law
:

Ideal Gas Mixture
:

Ideal Solution
:

Dew Pressure
: The pressure at which a vapor of a given composition and temperature begins to condense. The composition of the condensate is usually also calculated.

Bubble Pressure
: The pressure at which a liquid of a given composition and temperature begins to evaporate. The composition of the vapor is usually also calculated.

Dew Temperature
: The temperature at which a vapor of a given composition and pressure begins to condense. The composition of the condensate is usually also calculated.

Bubble Temperature
: The temperature at which a liquid of a given composition and pressure begins to evaporate. The composition of the vapor is usually also calculated.

Flash
: A mixture with a known overall composition is brought to a temperature and pressure in the two-phase region. The mixture separates into a vapor phase and a liquid phase. The compositions of the two phases and their relative amounts are usually calculated.

# Other Links and Videos

## Other Links

## Previous and Following Videos

### The video previous to the previous video (i.e., two videos back)

**Show Some Flash and Dew the Dew with a Little Bubbly!**<br/>*Graphical BUBL P, DEW P, BUBL T, DEW T, and FLASH calculations*<br/>[Video](https://youtu.be/shKvpcsJIEM), [Info Page](../../balances/material/GraphicalDewBubbleFlashVideoInfoWebPage.qmd), [Visuals](../../balances/material/72-BubbleDewFlashVisuals.qmd)

### The previous video (i.e., one video back)

**Oh, Henry!**<br/>*Gas-Liquid Equilibrium with one condensible component*<br/>[Video](https://youtu.be/dCOQy1C7yf0), [Info Page](../../balances/material/HenrysLawVideoInfoWebPage.qmd), [Visuals](../../balances/material/73-Visuals.qmd)

### The next video (i.e., one video forward)

**The Shocking Truth About Equilibrium Stages—Are You Doing It Wrong? Part 1**<br/>*Equilibrium Stage Equations*<br/>[Video](https://youtu.be/bY9fl5DJzmk), [Info Page](../../balances/material/EqullibriumStagesVideoInfoWebPage.qmd), [Visuals](../../balances/material/85-EquilibriumSeparationStages1Visuals.qmd)

### The next after next video (i.e. two videos forward)

**The Shocking Truth About Equilibrium Stages—Are You Doing It Wrong? Part 2**<br/>*Distillation Examples: Binary Phase Diagram, Raoult's law*<br/>[Video](https://youtu.be/wc6gv7lNtI0), [Info Page](../../balances/material/EqullibriumStagesVideoInfoWebPage.qmd), [Visuals](../../balances/material/86-EquilibriumSeparationStages2Visuals.qmd)
